signals.evidence: Mac Chew: 'unable to travel at the moment due to work commitments' — declined a procurement-event invite
Next Step: shortlist visibility pushed from April to 'third quarter'; FGV-FELDA merger + senior-team restructuring 'stretching' the process
CloseDate pushed 20 Sep->20 Nov (second push after 20 Jun->20 Sep on 03 May)
'New RFP received - need to requalify'; Zycus invited to bid, advanced Qualified->Formal Evaluation 26 Feb
SAP ECC is FGV's ERP of record; flagged 'our main competitor' (30 May 2024); positioned to bundle Ariba at a merger discount
3 years, no economic buyer reached; exec-connect (senior FGV sponsor -> Carl Kimball) still outstanding; KPMG is an access channel, not a champion
Amount raised 250k->350k->400k across 2026 (unilateral rep conviction; forecast still Pipeline)

top_rationale: Zycus is a live, buyer-included RFP participant (new RFP issued 29 Jan 2026, submitted 23 Feb, advanced to Formal Evaluation) - but the process has stalled: the shortlist FGV promised for April slipped to Q3 2026 amid the FGV-FELDA merger and senior-team restructuring.
Deal-content has been dark ~4.5 months; the only touch since is Mac Chew's 15 Jun 2026 cordial decline of a procurement-event invite - relationship-warm, not deal progress.
No economic buyer reached in 3 years; the exec-connect (Carl Kimball -> senior FGV sponsor) planned for mid-June is still outstanding and now overdue.
No internal champion - engagement is transactional; KPMG is an external access channel, not an advocate carrying Zycus vs SAP internally.
SAP is the incumbent ERP (SAP ECC), positioned to bundle Ariba at a merger discount - the one live competitive threat; Coupa/GEP/iValua are logged but dormant this sweep.

CRM shows unilateral rep conviction (amount 250k->400k) but two close-date pushes (Jun->Sep->Nov) and a Pipeline forecast; no Avoma calls exist, so recent deal-content evidence is thin (coverage partial).

drivers: ✅ Live RFP: FGV issued a new RFP (29 Jan 2026), Zycus submitted (23 Feb) and the deal advanced Qualified->Formal Evaluation - Zycus is a buyer-included, considered vendor, which is the one real asset holding this off the floor.

⚠️ No economic buyer reached in 3 years; the exec-connect (Carl -> senior FGV sponsor) planned for mid-June is still outstanding. ► Have Carl Kimball secure a 30-min session with a CPO/CFO-level sponsor before the Q3 shortlist.

⚠️ No internal champion - engagement is transactional and KPMG is only an access channel. ► Convert Mac Chew or Norzaida Jamal into an internal advocate (or recruit one) to carry Zycus vs SAP in the evaluation.

⚠️ SAP is the incumbent ERP (SAP ECC), positioned to bundle Ariba at a merger discount. ► Put Zycus's multi-entity, high-volume S2P depth (24 BUs, 60k+ RFx/yr) explicitly against a bolt-on ERP module before the merger renewal locks in.

⚠️ Stalled: April shortlist slipped to Q3, close pushed twice (Jun->Sep->Nov), deal-content dark since Feb. ► Get Norzaida to confirm dated shortlist criteria and a revised timeline to re-anchor the process.
⚠️ No quantified business case (metrics gap - only operational volumetrics). ► Build an ROI/savings model for a senior FGV audience to justify displacing SAP ECC.
win.focus_now: Reach a senior FGV economic buyer (via Carl) and put the anti-SAP S2P-depth differentiation in front of the evaluators before the Q3 shortlist - nothing advances without an exec sponsor and a reason to prefer Zycus over the incumbent.
win.ceiling_applied: None binding - stage ceiling (Formal Evaluation <=60) and Pipeline forecast cap (<=80) both sit above the score. The 21 is set by weak fundamentals (no EB, no champion, SAP incumbency, no quantified case) plus a ~4.5-month deal-content stall, not by a ceiling.

drivers: ✅ A written forward plan (Next Step 1 Jun 2026: exec-connect + Q3 shortlist visibility) and a dated future close (20 Nov 2026) give the deal benefit-of-the-doubt motion and hold the read at Steady rather than a hard stall.

⚠️ Underlying pulse is thin: the only recent buyer touch is Mac Chew's 15 Jun cordial decline of an event invite - not deal-content. ► Convert the open, cordial channel into a deal-content working session (shortlist criteria / SAP differentiation).
⚠️ Deal-content dark ~4.5 months (last real engagement = RFP submission 23 Feb 2026). ► Secure a dated buyer session before end of July to restart the pulse.
⚠️ Close date pushed twice (20 Jun->20 Sep->20 Nov). ► Re-baseline to a realistic Q1 2027 close and hold it rather than pushing again.
⚠️ Buyer is passive and the mid-June exec-connect is unconfirmed and now overdue. ► Have Carl confirm and complete the executive introduction this week.
⚠️ The April shortlist deadline passed in silence and slipped to a vague 'Q3'. ► Get Norzaida to commit a dated shortlist milestone so the process has a real forcing function.
mom.focus_now: Reviving-or-stalling hinges on one move: land a dated, deal-content buyer session in July (exec-connect or shortlist-criteria review) - right now the motion is a written plan, not buyer action.

zycus_commitments.item: Complete the executive introduction - connect a senior FGV sponsor (CPO/CFO-level) with Carl Kimball (VP APAC); overdue since mid-June, expedite.
zycus_commitments.grounding_quote: aim to connect exec with ck by mid June

waiting_on_buyer.item: RFP shortlist decision / announcement (promised April, slipped to Q3 2026)
Confirmed post-merger evaluation timeline and revised close expectation

best_practices.item: Reach a senior FGV economic buyer/sponsor via Carl Kimball (deal is single-threaded with no power sponsor; exec-connect is overdue and unblocks everything).
best_practices.rank_reason: Highest blocking-power x time-criticality - no EB in 3 years and the internal green-light doubt (Siti Balquish, 21 Mar 2025) both stem from missing executive sponsorship.

best_practices.item: Differentiate vs SAP: put Zycus's multi-entity, high-volume S2P depth explicitly against a bolt-on ERP module before SAP's merger-driven ERP renewal bundles Ariba at a discount.
best_practices.rank_reason: Active competitive deal-killer - the incumbent's merger-bundle window is the single largest loss risk and it has never been contested with the buyer directly.

best_practices.item: Re-engage on deal content: secure a dated shortlist-criteria / timeline review with Norzaida to restart the process and re-anchor the slipped shortlist.
best_practices.rank_reason: Deal-content dark ~4.5 months; a dated buyer session is the fastest way to convert a written plan into real motion.

best_practices.item: Recruit or develop an internal champion (Mac Chew or Norzaida) to advocate for Zycus internally; stop relying on KPMG as the only access channel.
best_practices.rank_reason: No champion in 3 years leaves Zycus unable to counter SAP inside FGV between touchpoints.

sum.headline: No change in the last 24h.
sum.as_of_note: As of 2026-07-09: last logged touch was 15 Jun 2026 (Mac Chew's cordial decline of a procurement-event invite - not deal-content); nothing in the 24-48h window. Deal-content has been dark since the 23 Feb 2026 RFP submission, and the April shortlist has slipped to Q3.
